evals.prompt: Tengo un proyecto Next.js en GitHub para un restaurante (lanonna.es). Quiero conectarlo a Vercel y configurar el dominio personalizado. El dominio está registrado en GoDaddy. ¿Cómo lo hago paso a paso? También quiero que www.lanonna.es redirija a lanonna.es.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step setup: create Vercel project (CLI or dashboard), link to GitHub repo, add domain in Vercel settings, DNS records to add at GoDaddy (A record 76.76.21.21 and CNAME for www), www→apex redirect config, and how to verify SSL + propagation.

assertions.description: Includes specific DNS records: A record pointing to 76.76.21.21 or CNAME to cname.vercel-dns.com for the root/www
Mentions that SSL certificate is automatically provisioned by Vercel after DNS resolves
Explains how to set up www.lanonna.es → lanonna.es redirect (either via Vercel dashboard or next.config.ts redirects)
Provides numbered or clearly ordered setup steps (not just a list of unordered tips)
Mentions DNS propagation time (can take minutes to 48 hours) and how to check status

evals.prompt: Soy una agencia con 5 clientes distintos, cada uno con su propio proyecto Next.js en Vercel. Tengo 2 desarrolladores. ¿Qué plan de Vercel necesito? ¿Cómo gestiono el equipo y el acceso? Y los previews de los clientes, ¿puedo protegerlos con contraseña para que solo ellos los vean?
evals.expected_output: Pro plan recommendation with justification (commercial use requirement, team features, password protection). Team setup instructions, member roles (Owner/Member/Viewer), cost optimization tip (one team for all clients shares bandwidth/minutes), and deployment protection / password protection setup.

assertions.description: Recommends the Pro plan (not Hobby) with justification for commercial/agency use
Notes that Hobby plan cannot be used for commercial work (client projects) per Vercel ToS
Explains team member roles: Owner (billing access), Member (deploy/configure), and/or Viewer (read-only)
Mentions password protection for preview deployments (Deployment Protection in Vercel settings)
Mentions that one Vercel team can host all client projects, sharing bandwidth and build minutes
